gov_command_output.route_rationale: ESCALATE verdict and SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN binding are correct per OPEN-082-TRAP; gate failure is FORMAT-ORIENTED not SUBSTANTIVE. Both workers correctly preserved core source bindings. Worker 1 suffered missing-critical-term failures (Iris Installers, Site 4Q, umbrella liability, currently bound|CE-12). Worker 2 achieved 11 critical features but failed on action_boundary_verdict_mismatch, indicating source-boundary mapping articulation gap. Gov blocks: (1) inverting ESCALATE to ALLOW, (2) treating END-IRI-089 as sufficient authority without status-class confirmation, (3) dismissing WARN-IRI-089 as advisory, (4) accepting abstract coverage without insurance-status class confirmation, (5) allowing ALLOW before status-class dependency resolves. Gov preserves: ESCALATE verdict, SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN binding, status-class trap per WARN-IRI-089, policy exact-match rule per STD-IRI-089-P, the five-element requirement (contractor|Site|coverage|work_date|status_class), CE-12 as operative carrier notation.

next_worker_baton.objective: Repair action_boundary_verdict_mismatch by articulating the source-boundary mapping precisely. The action_boundary is 'May facilities allow contractor site work to begin?' The policy STD-IRI-089-P requires exact match across five elements: contractor, site, coverage type, work start date, and insurance-status class. The first four are satisfied by END-IRI-089 and WORK-IRI-089. The fifth—insurance-status class confirmation—is blocked by WARN-IRI-089, which flags COI staleness and mandates separate verification. CE-12 notation in END-IRI-089 indicates coverage-bound status but NOT insurance-status class confirmation per WARN-IRI-089. Deliver ESCALATE with explicit boundary mapping showing which element is unresolvable under current source records.
next_worker_baton.attack_focus: Challenge any attempt to treat END-IRI-089 as status-class confirmation. Challenge any treatment of WARN-IRI-089 as advisory rather than operative blocker. Verify that CE-12 notation is referenced correctly as 'currently-bound carrier endorsement' per END-IRI-089 but NOT conflated with insurance-status class confirmation per WARN-IRI-089. Maintain source-boundary alignment across all five required elements. Articulate why the action_boundary question cannot be answered ALLOW under current source state.
required_repairs: Include all four critical source identifiers explicitly: 'Iris Installers', 'Site 4Q', 'umbrella liability', 'currently-bound carrier notation CE-12'

Articulate action_boundary_verdict mapping: show how the question 'May facilities allow contractor site work to begin?' maps to the five-element policy requirement and which element remains unresolved
Differentiate coverage-bound endorsement (CE-12 in END-IRI-089) from insurance-status class confirmation (required by STD-IRI-089-P and flagged as absent by WARN-IRI-089)
Ensure WARN-IRI-089 is cited as operative open-blocker, not advisory
Demonstrate monotonic_preservation: ESCALATE verdict, SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN binding, OPEN-082-TRAP status, the five-element requirement, CE-12 as carrier notation only
monotonic_preservation: STD-IRI-089-P as exact-match policy requiring five elements

internal_documents.content: Work start WORK-IRI-089 begins for Iris Installers at Site 4Q on 2026-06-28 requiring umbrella liability.
Insurance warning WARN-IRI-089 says some COI records are stale and insurance status must be separately verified.
Endorsement END-IRI-089 covers Iris Installers, Site 4Q, umbrella liability, currently bound carrier endorsement CE-12, effective before work begins.
